language_boundary: IEC 61508-aligned terminology only. No IEC 61508 compliance, certification, or SIL claim is made. See docs/IEC_61508_ROADMAP.md for the assessment gap analysis.

hazards.description: Actuator command exceeds the configured actuator limit, risking coil over-drive and loss of vertical control authority.
A control action executes while its paired safety limit is violated.
An internal Petri marking leaves the declared per-place capacity bounds, producing undefined controller state.
The compiled SNN enables an action path that the source Petri net forbids.
Control decisions are computed from an equilibrium case that failed input validation.
A diverging phase-control trajectory continues to actuate without refusal.
A critical physics-invariant violation fails to trigger disruption mitigation.
A control episode cannot be reproduced deterministically for post-event audit.

requirements.statement: Normalised PID actuator command magnitudes never exceed the configured actuator limit and never amplify the raw command unless saturating to that limit.
requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-3 Table A.4 formal methods language (aligned terminology only).
gaps: The Lean model proves saturation of normalised natural-number magnitudes; a signed physical-unit coil-current saturation proof is a declared next target (README Formal Verification section).

requirements.statement: Each declared safety channel inhibits its paired control transition, and combined violations disable all control actions.
requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-1 safety-function allocation language (aligned terminology only).
gaps: Interlock-semantics preservation through Petri-to-SNN compilation is the declared M-1 Lean proof target; today the guarantee is test- and replay-based.

requirements.statement: Petri markings stay within declared per-place capacity bounds under assignment and repeated firing updates.

public_claim.pattern: finite-capacity Petri token filters preserve per-place bounds

requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-3 Table A.4 formal methods language (aligned terminology only).

requirements.statement: Petri-to-SNN compilation introduces no action path absent from the source net: every compiled direct edge and reachable path corresponds to a Petri edge or declared topology.

public_claim.pattern: no compiled SNN direct edge or reachable path exists without a corresponding Petri edge

requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-3 tool-qualification and translation-integrity language (aligned terminology only).

requirements.statement: Invalid Grad-Shafranov case descriptions fail closed: the solver propagates the validation error and performs no numerical work or state mutation.

public_claim.pattern: Grad-Shafranov validation errors are propagated exactly

requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-3 defensive-programming and input-validation language (aligned terminology only).

requirements.statement: The Lyapunov guard refuses phase-control actuation when the largest Lyapunov exponent estimate indicates a diverging trajectory.
gaps: The refusal logic is empirically tested; no formal proof of the divergence estimator exists yet.

requirements.statement: Any critical physics-invariant violation (including non-finite observables) triggers the disruption-mitigation path.
requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-1 safe-state transition language (aligned terminology only).
gaps: Mitigation triggering is contract- and test-based; no formal proof covers the invariant evaluator.

requirements.statement: A full control episode replays bit-identically on the generating environment class; across differing CPU microarchitectures the asserted invariant is the transcendental-free disruption indicator component.

public_claim.pattern: Identical inputs produce identical outputs across platforms

requirements.iec61508_alignment: IEC 61508-3 audit-trail and reproducibility language (aligned terminology only).
gaps: Cross-machine bit-identity of transcendental components is environment-conditional: numpy dispatches SIMD exp/sin kernels per CPU microarchitecture (first two-machine comparison, CI run 28841804121). Replay-invariance formalisation is the declared M-1 proof target.
